objects.name: Analytic 1712
objects.description: Correlates (1) application access to or staging of local files likely to be of operational, evidentiary, or user value, (2) deletion of those files or wipe-like destructive actions through ordinary storage access, administrative controls, or privileged/rooted paths, and (3) continued app or device activity after deletion, including cleanup, concealment, or outbound transfer. The defender observes a causal chain where files are first accessed or prepared, then removed, and device-side behavior continues after evidence or data is gone.

x_mitre_log_source_references.name: android:MDMLog
x_mitre_log_source_references.channel: application holds device administrator, device owner, or other managed authority capable of wipe or destructive device-level action before bulk file loss or wipe event

x_mitre_log_source_references.name: android:MDMLog
x_mitre_log_source_references.channel: device posture indicates rooted, compromised, or non-compliant state before protected or atypical filesystem deletion activity

x_mitre_log_source_references.name: MobileEDR:telemetry
x_mitre_log_source_references.channel: application deletes, truncates, or removes user, operational, or evidence-bearing files after prior access or staging and before later continued execution or communication

x_mitre_log_source_references.name: MobileEDR:telemetry
x_mitre_log_source_references.channel: application invokes file-management, package, storage, or administrative wipe operations immediately before loss of expected local files or file collections

x_mitre_mutable_elements.description: Correlation window between file access or staging, deletion event, and subsequent activity
File paths, storage scopes, and data classes monitored for suspicious deletion, such as documents, databases, media, email stores, or update artifacts
Threshold for number, size, or concentration of deleted files required before escalation
Legitimate applications expected to rotate, purge, or clean up files in the environment
Administrative or rooted control paths that materially increase destructive file deletion capability
Outbound traffic threshold used to distinguish exfiltration-linked cleanup from benign maintenance activity
